event: end-stage kidney disease of unknown origin since 1989
cranial CT scan showed several contrast-enhancing intracerebral lesions
cranial MRI scan showed several contrast-enhancing intracerebral lesions
transmitted to the university department of Nephrology
transmitted to the department of Neurosurgery
cerebral PTLD (diffuse large B-cell lymphoma positive for Epstein-Barr virus) diagnosed
no additional manifestations of lymphoma on CT scan of the thorax
no additional manifestations of lymphoma on abdomen sonography
no additional manifestations of lymphoma on bone marrow biopsy
high-dose cytarabin 3 g/qm intravenous (cycle 1 of five cycles)
Rituximab 375 mg/qm intravenous (cycle 1 of five cycles)
five cycles high-dose cytarabin and Rituximab completed
MRI confirmed complete remission of PTLD
chemotherapy changed to Rituximab only 500 mg/qm
mycofenolate sodium tapered to 360 mg bid
cerebral MRI scan demonstrated recurrence of PTLD
baseline kidney transplant function markedly reduced
no evidence of acute kidney injury
MTX level in serum no longer measurable
no acute kidney failure after discontinuing dialysis
no significant rebound of MTX level after dialysis
no changes in estimated glomerular filtration rate after termination of dialysis
preexisting anemia did not impair significantly
no changes in the number of thrombocytes occurred
did not require granulocyte colony-stimulating factor
transmitted to an intensive care unit
follow up cerebral MRI scan showed small regredience of PTLD
no remission of PTLD with HDMTX
no relevant response of the disease after radiotherapy
